verses.text: The burden of Babylon: a banner is lifted, and the LORD musters armies from a far country, 'the weapons of his indignation,' to destroy the land. God summons the host. The mightiest empire is no match for the assembled judgment of God.

'Howl ye; for the day of the LORD is at hand'; all hands grow faint and hearts melt in anguish like a woman in travail. Terror seizes the proud city. When God's day of reckoning comes, no courage can stand against it.

'Howl ye; for the day of the LORD is at hand'; all hands grow faint and hearts melt in anguish like a woman in travail. Terror seizes the proud city. When God's day of reckoning comes, no courage can stand against it.

'Howl ye; for the day of the LORD is at hand'; all hands grow faint and hearts melt in anguish like a woman in travail. Terror seizes the proud city. When God's day of reckoning comes, no courage can stand against it.

The day of the LORD comes cruel, the sun, moon, and stars darkened, to punish the world for its evil and lay low the arrogance of the proud. The heavens themselves seem to shudder. God's judgment shakes the whole order men trusted in.

Each flees toward his own land like a hunted gazelle, while the cruelty of war falls on the helpless. Babylon's victims suffer her own measure. The violence the proud city dealt out returns upon her in full.

Each flees toward his own land like a hunted gazelle, while the cruelty of war falls on the helpless. Babylon's victims suffer her own measure. The violence the proud city dealt out returns upon her in full.

God stirs up the Medes, who care not for silver, and Babylon, 'the glory of kingdoms,' shall be overthrown 'as... Sodom and Gomorrah.' Her splendour ends in ruin. The greatest of empires falls when God decrees it.

God stirs up the Medes, who care not for silver, and Babylon, 'the glory of kingdoms,' shall be overthrown 'as... Sodom and Gomorrah.' Her splendour ends in ruin. The greatest of empires falls when God decrees it.

Babylon shall never be inhabited again; wild beasts and owls shall haunt her palaces, 'her time is near to come.' Desolation is complete and lasting. The proud city becomes a wilderness, a warning to all who trust in earthly glory.
